warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — PASS
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — PASS

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for furan were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). The geometry was the user-supplied furan.xyz (fixed; no re-optimization). Three SCFs were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges used the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (most electrophilic site): atom 5 (C), f+ = 1.9242931. The integrity check passed: status=ok, trustworthy=true; sum(f+) = sum(f-) = 1.0000. All three integrity checks passed. No engine warnings were issued. The dual descriptor confirms C4 and C5 as the primary electrophilic/nucleophilic sites; oxygen (atom 1) shows a negative dual descriptor consistent with HOMO-dominated (nucleophilic) character. Negative Mulliken f+ on hydrogen atoms are a known artifact of Mulliken population analysis.
